title: IngressRoute vs Kubernetes Ingress
description:
## IngressRoute vs Kubernetes Ingress

Traefik supports both the standard Kubernetes `Ingress` resource and its own custom `IngressRoute` CRD.

Which statement correctly identifies an advantage of `IngressRoute` over a standard `Ingress`?

options.text: IngressRoute is easier to configure because it uses YAML annotations instead of a spec block
IngressRoute supports native middleware composition, TCP/UDP routing, and priority-based rule ordering — features not expressible in standard Ingress
IngressRoute automatically provisions TLS certificates from Let's Encrypt without any configuration
IngressRoute is a cluster-scoped resource, while Ingress is namespace-scoped

explanation: The standard Kubernetes Ingress spec is intentionally minimal — complex features require non-portable annotations. Traefik's `IngressRoute` CRD expresses routing rules natively in typed YAML: middleware chains (auth, rate-limit, strip-prefix), TCP/UDP routes, weighted load balancing, and route priority. Let's Encrypt requires a separate ACME resolver configuration in both Ingress and IngressRoute. Both resource types are namespace-scoped.

hints.title: Listing IngressRoutes
hints.body: IngressRoute is a Traefik CRD. Use kubectl to list them the same way you would any other resource.
